description: Migration up adds activity_type, acknowledged_at, acknowledged_by, occurrence_count, last_occurrence_at, matched_rule_id, auto_ticket_created to rmm_alerts
Migration up adds dedup_key and the (tenant, integration_id, dedup_key) index
Migration up adds conditions/actions jsonb and drops all eleven flat columns from rmm_alert_rules
Migration down restores the prior rmm_alerts and rmm_alert_rules schema
Unit: NinjaOne TRIGGERED payload maps to a NormalizedRmmAlertEvent with all fields populated
Unit: NinjaOne severity values (CRITICAL/MAJOR/MODERATE/MINOR/NONE) normalize to internal severities
Unit: condition identity uses statusCode and falls back to activityType when statusCode is absent
Unit: NinjaOne RESET and ACKNOWLEDGED payloads map to kind reset / acknowledged
Unit: TacticalRMM alert payload maps to a NormalizedRmmAlertEvent
Triggered event inserts an rmm_alerts row with the raw payload in metadata (no source_data writes anywhere)
Replaying the same external_alert_id updates the existing row instead of inserting a duplicate
dedup_key is computed and stored on insert
Rule with lower priority_order wins when multiple rules match
Inactive rules are never evaluated
Rules from a different integration or tenant are never evaluated
severities condition matches and mismatches correctly
activityTypes condition matches and mismatches correctly
alertClasses and sourceTypes conditions match and mismatch correctly
organizationIds condition filters by the alert's external organization
keywords condition does substring matching on the alert message
messagePattern regex matches the alert message
A rule with an invalid stored regex is skipped with a log entry and the next rule is evaluated
A rule with empty conditions matches every alert
A rule with multiple condition fields requires all of them to match
matched_rule_id is stored on the alert row
No matching rule stores the alert with no ticket created
Matched rule with createTicket false stores the alert with no ticket created
First trigger for a dedup_key creates a ticket
Repeat trigger while the linked ticket is open creates no new ticket, increments occurrence_count, updates last_occurrence_at
Repeat trigger adds the 're-triggered — Nth occurrence' internal comment
Trigger after the linked ticket is closed creates a new ticket
Same condition on a different device creates a separate ticket
boardId, priorityOverride, and assignToUserId from the rule are applied to the created ticket
Severity-to-priority fallback applies when the rule has no priorityOverride
Title/description templates render all four placeholders
Default title and description are used when the rule has no template
Created ticket has source, source_reference, an asset association, and an initial internal comment
Ticket client comes from the asset when the device is mapped
Ticket client falls back to the organization mapping when no asset is mapped
auto_ticket_created is true and ticket_id is set on the alert row after auto-creation
Reset marks the alert resolved with resolved_at
Reset with autoResolveTicket adds a resolution comment to the linked ticket
Reset without autoResolveTicket leaves the linked ticket without comment or status change
Untouched ticket closes on reset using the tenant's first is_closed status by default
autoResolveStatusId is honored when set on the rule
Ticket with a human comment stays open on reset
Ticket with a time entry stays open on reset
Ticket with a manual status change stays open on reset
Rule-driven auto-assignment alone still counts as untouched
Acknowledged event sets status acknowledged with acknowledged_at
Closing an alert-linked ticket calls the outbound adapter when resetAlertOnTicketClose is true
Closing the ticket makes no outbound call when the rule set resetAlertOnTicketClose false
Closing a ticket with no linked alerts is a no-op for the subscriber
Closing a ticket whose alert is already resolved makes no outbound call
Outbound reset failure is logged, stamped in alert metadata, and the ticket close still succeeds
Provider without an outbound adapter is skipped cleanly on ticket close
E2E: NinjaOne TRIGGERED webhook for a mapped org produces an rmm_alerts row and a ticket
E2E: NinjaOne RESET webhook resolves the alert and auto-closes the untouched ticket
E2E: the same webhook delivered twice is a no-op the second time
Webhook for an unmapped organization returns 200 and processes nothing (existing behavior preserved)
Webhook with an invalid signature/secret is rejected (existing behavior preserved)
Unexpected pipeline error returns 500 so the RMM retries
E2E: Tactical alert webhook flows through the shared pipeline and creates a ticket
listRmmAlertRules returns the integration's rules ordered by priority_order
createRmmAlertRule persists valid conditions/actions
createRmmAlertRule rejects malformed conditions/actions shapes
createRmmAlertRule rejects an invalid messagePattern regex
updateRmmAlertRule modifies a rule and re-validates shapes
deleteRmmAlertRule removes the rule
reorderRmmAlertRules persists the new priority_order across rules
Non-admin users are denied on every rule CRUD action
Rule CRUD cannot read or modify another tenant's rules
UI: Alert Rules section renders in NinjaOne integration settings
UI: Alert Rules section renders in TacticalRMM integration settings
UI: creating a rule through the dialog adds it to the list
UI: Match group inputs round-trip (save then reopen shows the same values)
UI: organization picker lists the integration's mapped organizations
UI: Actions group inputs round-trip including both lifecycle toggles and notify-users
UI: template inputs show placeholder hints
UI: active toggle enables/disables a rule from the list
UI: reordering rules persists and changes evaluation order
UI: invalid regex shows an inline validation error and blocks save
UI: deleting a rule requires confirmation
RMM_ALERT_TRIGGERED is present in the workflow v2 catalog with its payload schema
RMM_ALERT_RESOLVED is present in the workflow v2 catalog
Pipeline publishes RMM_ALERT_TRIGGERED with alert, asset/client, and ticket IDs in the payload
Pipeline publishes RMM_ALERT_RESOLVED on reset
A workflow with an RMM_ALERT_TRIGGERED trigger fires when an alert arrives
rmm.alerts.create_ticket workflow action creates a ticket for an unlinked alert
rmm.alerts.create_ticket errors cleanly when the alert already has a ticket
Matched rule notifyUserIds produces an in-app notification for each user
Email notification is sent to users with the rmm-alert category enabled and suppressed for users who opted out
OAuth callback rejects a state payload with a wrong or expired csrf token
OAuth callback accepts a valid state payload
No imports of the old ee/server ninjaone/alerts modules remain
An org mapping with auto_create_tickets false still gets tickets when a rule matches (flag no longer consulted)
